subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Electrical Injuries
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Electrical Injuries.

hook.text: Electricity follows the path of least resistance through the body—often through the heart.

buildup.text: Electrical current causes burns at entry and exit points, but the worst damage is internal.

discovery.text: Current passing through the heart can cause fatal arrhythmias even at relatively low voltages.

twist.text: The small burns visible on the skin often hide massive internal tissue destruction along the current path.

climax.text: All electrical injury victims need cardiac monitoring—the heart can develop problems hours later.

punchline.text: The surface burn is nothing. The damage inside along the path is all.

quiz.question: Why do electrical injuries require cardiac monitoring?
options: Current through the heart can cause delayed fatal arrhythmias
Electricity always stops the heart immediately
